3hr.pr.notes: at surface; includes both liquid and solid phases.  This is the 3-hour mean precipitation flux.

6hrPlev.hurs.notes: The relative humidity with respect to liquid water for T> 0 C, and with respect to ice for T<0 C.

6hrPlevPt.ua.notes: Zonal wind (positive in a eastward direction).

6hrPlevPt.va.notes: Meridional wind (positive in a northward direction).

Amon.cl.notes: Includes both large-scale and convective cloud.

Amon.cli.notes: Includes both large-scale and convective cloud.  This is calculated as the mass of cloud ice in the grid cell divided by the mass of air (including the water in all phases) in the grid cell. It includes precipitating hydrometeors ONLY if the precipitating hydrometeors affect the calculation of radiative transfer in model.

Amon.clivi.notes: mass of ice water in the column divided by the area of the column (not just the area of the cloudy portion of the column). Includes precipitating frozen hydrometeors ONLY if the precipitating hydrometeor affects the calculation of radiative transfer in model.

Amon.clt.notes: for the whole atmospheric column, as seen from the surface or the top of the atmosphere. Include both large-scale and convective cloud.

Amon.clw.description: Mass Fraction of Cloud Liquid Water
Amon.clw.notes: Includes both large-scale and convective cloud.  Calculate as the mass of cloud liquid water in the grid cell divided by the mass of air (including the water in all phases) in the grid cells. Precipitating hydrometeors are included ONLY if the precipitating hydrometeors affect the calculation of radiative transfer in model.

Amon.clwvi.notes: mass of condensed (liquid + ice) water in the column divided by the area of the column (not just the area of the cloudy portion of the column). Includes precipitating hydrometeors ONLY if the precipitating hydrometeor affects the calculation of radiative transfer in model.

Amon.evspsbl.notes: at surface; flux of water into the atmosphere due to conversion of both liquid and solid phases to vapor (from underlying surface and vegetation)

Amon.hfss.notes: The surface sensible heat flux, also called turbulent heat flux, is the exchange of heat between the surface and the air by motion of air.

Amon.hur.notes: This is the relative humidity with respect to liquid water for T> 0 C, and with respect to ice for T<0 C.

Amon.hurs.notes: This is the relative humidity with respect to liquid water for T> 0 C, and with respect to ice for T<0 C.

Amon.hus.notes: Specific humidity is the mass fraction of water vapor in (moist) air.

Amon.pr.notes: at surface; includes both liquid and solid phases from all types of clouds (both large-scale and convective)

Amon.prc.notes: at surface; includes both liquid and solid phases.

Amon.prsn.notes: at surface; includes precipitation of all forms of water in the solid phase

Amon.prw.notes: Vertically integrated mass of water vapour through the atmospheric column

Amon.ps.notes: not, in general, the same as mean sea-level pressure

Amon.psl.notes: not, in general, the same as surface pressure

Amon.rlds.notes: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. "longwave" means longwave radiation. Downwelling radiation is radiation from above. It does not mean "net downward".

Amon.rlus.notes: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. "longwave" means longwave radiation. Upwelling radiation is radiation from below. It does not mean "net upward".

Amon.rlut.notes: at the top of the atmosphere (to be compared with satellite measurements)

Amon.rlutcs.notes: Upwelling clear-sky longwave radiation at top of atmosphere

Amon.rsds.notes: Surface solar irradiance for UV calculations.

Amon.rsdscs.notes: Surface solar irradiance clear sky for UV calculations

Amon.rsdt.notes: at the top of the atmosphere

Amon.rsus.notes: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. "shortwave" means shortwave radiation. Upwelling radiation is radiation from below. It does not mean "net upward".

Amon.rsut.notes: at the top of the atmosphere

Amon.rsutcs.notes: Calculated in the absence of clouds.

Amon.sfcWind.notes: This is the mean of the speed, not the speed computed from the mean u and v components of wind

Amon.tasmax.notes: monthly mean of the daily-maximum near-surface air temperature.

Amon.tasmin.notes: monthly mean of the daily-minimum near-surface air temperature.

Amon.tauu.notes: Downward eastward wind stress at the surface

Amon.tauv.notes: Downward northward wind stress at the surface

Amon.ua.notes: Zonal wind (positive in a eastward direction).

Amon.uas.notes: Eastward component of the near-surface (usually, 10 meters)  wind

Amon.va.notes: Meridional wind (positive in a northward direction).

Amon.vas.notes: Northward component of the near surface wind

Amon.wap.notes: commonly referred to as "omega", this represents the vertical component of velocity in pressure coordinates (positive down)

Amon.zg.notes: Geopotential height is the geopotential divided by the standard acceleration due to gravity.

CFday.ps.notes: surface pressure (not mean sea-level pressure), 2-D field to calculate the 3-D pressure field from hybrid coordinates

day.clt.notes: for the whole atmospheric column, as seen from the surface or the top of the atmosphere. Includes both large-scale and convective cloud.

day.hur.notes: This is the relative humidity with respect to liquid water for T> 0 C, and with respect to ice for T<0 C.

day.hurs.notes: This is the relative humidity with respect to liquid water for T> 0 C, and with respect to ice for T<0 C.

day.hus.notes: Specific humidity is the mass fraction of water vapor in (moist) air.

day.pr.notes: at surface; includes both liquid and solid phases from all types of clouds (both large-scale and convective)

day.rsds.notes: Surface solar irradiance for UV calculations.

day.ua.notes: Zonal wind (positive in a eastward direction).

day.uas.notes: Eastward component of the near-surface (usually, 10 meters)  wind

day.va.notes: Meridional wind (positive in a northward direction).

day.vas.notes: Northward component of the near surface wind

day.wap.notes: commonly referred to as "omega", this represents the vertical component of velocity in pressure coordinates (positive down)

day.zg.notes: Geopotential height is the geopotential divided by the standard acceleration due to gravity.

fx.areacella.description: Grid-Cell Area for Atmospheric Grid Variables
fx.areacella.notes: Cell areas for any grid used to report atmospheric variables and any other variable using that grid.

fx.mrsofc.description: Capacity of Soil to Store Water (Field Capacity)

fx.rootd.notes: report the maximum soil depth reachable by plant roots.

fx.sftgif.notes: fraction of grid cell occupied by "permanent" ice (i.e., glaciers).

fx.sftlf.description: Percentage of the Grid Cell Occupied by Land (Including Lakes)
fx.sftlf.notes: Percentage of horizontal area occupied by land.

LImon.snc.notes: Fraction of each grid cell that is occupied by snow that rests on land portion of cell.

LImon.snw.notes: Computed as the mass of surface snow on the land portion of the grid cell divided by the land area in the grid cell.

Lmon.evspsblveg.notes: the canopy evaporation+sublimation (if present in model).

Lmon.lai.notes: A ratio obtained by dividing the total upper leaf surface area of vegetation by the horizontal surface area of the land.

Lmon.mrfso.notes: the mass (summed over all all layers) of frozen water.

Lmon.mrro.notes: the total runoff leaving the land portion of the grid cell.

Lmon.mrros.notes: the total surface runoff leaving the land portion of the grid cell.

Lmon.mrso.notes: the mass per unit area (summed over all soil layers) of water in all phases.

Lmon.mrsos.description: Moisture in Upper Portion of Soil Column
Lmon.mrsos.notes: the mass of water in all phases in a thin surface soil layer.

Oday.sos.notes: Sea water salinity is the salt content of sea water.

Oday.tos.notes: This may differ from surface temperature in regions of sea ice or floating ice shelves.

Oday.zos.notes: This is the effective dynamic sea level.

Ofx.areacello.notes: Cell areas for any grid used to report ocean variables.

Ofx.basin.notes: A variable with the standard name of region contains strings which indicate geographical regions.

Ofx.hfgeou.description: Upward Geothermal Heat Flux at Sea Floor
Ofx.hfgeou.notes: Upward geothermal heat flux per unit area on the sea floor

Ofx.masscello.notes: Tracer grid-cell mass per unit area used for computing tracer budgets.

Ofx.sftof.notes: This is the area fraction at the ocean surface.

Omon.bigthetao.notes: Diagnostic should be contributed only for models using conservative temperature as prognostic field.

Omon.hfds.description: Downward Heat Flux at Sea Water Surface
Omon.hfds.notes: This is the net flux of heat entering the liquid water column through its upper surface.

Omon.masscello.notes: For Boussinesq models, report this diagnostic as Boussinesq reference density times grid cell volume.

Omon.mlotst.description: Ocean Mixed Layer Thickness Defined by Delta Sigma T of 0.03 kg m-3 referenced to the model level closest to 10 m depth
Omon.mlotst.notes: Defined by Sigma T of 0.03 kg m-3 wrt to model level closest to 10 m depth.

Omon.so.notes: Sea water salinity is the salt content of sea water.

Omon.sos.notes: Sea water salinity is the salt content of sea water.

Omon.tauuo.description: Sea Water Surface Downward X Stress
Omon.tauuo.notes: This is the stress on the liquid ocean from overlying atmosphere, sea ice, ice shelf, etc.

Omon.tauvo.description: Sea Water Surface Downward Y Stress
Omon.tauvo.notes: This is the stress on the liquid ocean from overlying atmosphere, sea ice, ice shelf, etc.

Omon.thetao.notes: Diagnostic should be contributed even for models using conservative temperature as prognostic field.

Omon.thkcello.notes: The time varying thickness of ocean cells.

Omon.tos.notes: This may differ from surface temperature in regions of sea ice or floating ice shelves.

Omon.umo.notes: X-ward mass transport from residual mean advective transport.

Omon.uo.notes: Prognostic x-ward velocity component resolved by the model.

Omon.vmo.notes: Y-ward mass transport from residual mean advective transport.

Omon.vo.notes: Prognostic y-ward velocity component resolved by the model.

Omon.wmo.notes: Upward mass transport from residual mean advective transport.

Omon.wo.notes: Prognostic z-ward velocity component resolved by the model.

Omon.zos.notes: This is the effective dynamic sea level.

Omon.zostoga.description: Global Average Thermosteric Sea Level Change
Omon.zostoga.notes: There is no CMIP6 request for zosga nor zossga.

SIday.siconc.notes: Percentage of a given grid cell that is covered by sea ice on the ocean grid.

SImon.siconc.notes: Percentage of a given grid cell that is covered by sea ice on the ocean grid.

SImon.simass.notes: Total mass of sea ice divided by grid-cell area.

SImon.sisnthick.notes: Actual thickness of snow averaged over the snow-covered part of the sea ice.

SImon.sitemptop.notes: Mean surface temperature of the sea-ice covered part of the grid cell.

SImon.sithick.notes: Actual thickness of sea ice averaged over the ice-covered part of a given grid cell.

SImon.sitimefrac.description: Fraction of Time Steps with Sea Ice
SImon.sitimefrac.notes: Fraction of time steps of the averaging period during which sea ice is present.

SImon.siu.notes: X-component of sea-ice velocity on native model grid.

SImon.siv.notes: Y-component of sea-ice velocity on native model grid.
